title: Accelerator Grid Life Modeling of T6 Ion Thruster for BepiColombo
authors_or_org: Vernon H. Chaplin, Dan M. Goebel (NASA Jet Propulsion Laboratory / Caltech), Rhodri A. Lewis, Francis Lockwood Estrin, Peter N. Randall (Airbus Defence and Space, UK, per T6/QinetiQ EP heritage; affiliation not independently re-confirmed this session)
claim_supported: Models accelerator-grid erosion/life for QinetiQ/Airbus's T6 Kaufman-type ion thruster (5kW-class, powered by a dedicated PPU) as qualified for ESA's BepiColombo mission to Mercury, a distinct thruster/grid-life sub-niche from this lane's existing Hall-thruster-PPU-heavy record set.
locator: Journal of Propulsion and Power (AIAA), Vol.37, No.3, pp.436-449 (2021)
notes: Metadata (full author list, container-title, volume/issue/pages, year, type=journal-article) confirmed directly via Crossref API (api.crossref.org/works/10.2514/1.B37938) this session; the AIAA arc.aiaa.org abstract page itself was not separately fetched. Geography GB is inferred from the T6/QinetiQ-Airbus EP-program heritage for the non-JPL co-authors, not independently confirmed from an affiliation list this session -- flag for verifier. New sub-niche vs. existing ledger: European (ESA/Airbus) ion-thruster grid-life modeling, distinct from L09-002/L09-006/L09-007/L09-008 (Japanese Hall-thruster PPU topology) and L09-013 (Brayton-cycle nuclear power).

title: Improved control architecture and strategy for iodine ion thruster following in-orbit demonstration and system-level radiation testing
authors_or_org: A. Bore, P. Proynov, D. Rafalskyi (ThrustMe, Massy, France)
claim_supported: Describes control-architecture and control-strategy improvements for ThrustMe's NPT30-I2 iodine-fueled ion-thruster/PPU system, informed directly by in-orbit flight demonstration data and a dedicated system-level radiation-test campaign -- a distinct propellant (iodine, not xenon) and power-control sub-niche versus this lane's existing xenon Hall/ion-thruster PPU records.
locator: Journal of Electric Propulsion (Springer), Vol.2, No.1, article 7 (2023)
notes: Full author list, container-title, volume/issue/article-number, year confirmed directly via Crossref API this session. Geography FR based on ThrustMe's Massy, France headquarters (standard company location, not independently re-verified from the article's own affiliation block this session). New sub-niche: iodine-propellant EP power/control with radiation-test data, not previously represented in the L09 ledger (existing iodine mentions were none).

title: Heavy-Ion Irradiation Response of Gallium Nitride High-Electron-Mobility Transistors with Reduced Surface Field Technology
authors_or_org: Wiley Yu, Jeong-Kyu Kim, Thomas Andres Rodriguez, Rohith Soman, Srabanti Chowdhury (Stanford University)
claim_supported: Reports heavy-ion (single-event-effect-relevant) irradiation response of GaN HEMTs built with RESURF (reduced-surface-field) technology -- a device-architecture-specific primary radiation-test result distinct from this lane's existing GaN records (L09-020's neutron+gamma TID/displacement-damage synergy study and L09-014's general review), addressing the single-event side of the GaN space-qualification gap flagged in this lane's prior scout notes.
locator: physica status solidi (a), Vol.223, Issue 1, article e202500589 (2026)
notes: Full author list, container-title, volume/issue, year confirmed directly via Crossref API this session. Geography US inferred from Srabanti Chowdhury's known Stanford University GaN-power-device lab affiliation; not independently re-confirmed from the article's own affiliation block this session -- flag for verifier.

title: Single Event Effects of SiC Power MOSFETs: From Neutron Interaction to Destruction at the Die Level
authors_or_org: Rosine Coq Germanicus, Alain Michez, Kimmo Niskanen, Mahima Chaudhary, Guillaume Bascoul, Vanessa Chazal, Frederic Wrobel, Jerome Boch
claim_supported: Combines heavy-ion and neutron-secondary-ion TCAD single-event-effect simulation with experimental irradiation (ChipIr, Rutherford Appleton Laboratory) of a commercial 4H-SiC power MOSFET, characterizing single-event burnout/gate-rupture destruction mechanisms at the die level -- a primary (non-review) SiC radiation-reliability study directly relevant to qualifying SiC power converters for spacecraft power electronics, complementing this lane's GaN-focused radiation records.
locator: IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science, Vol.72, Issue 8, pp.2368-2376 (2025)
notes: IEEE Xplore abstract page returned blank content to direct WebFetch this session; full author list, container-title (confirmed as the IEEE Transactions on Nuclear Science journal, not a conference), volume/issue/pages, and year were independently confirmed via Crossref API (api.crossref.org/works/10.1109/TNS.2025.3561583). Geography FR inferred from author-institution pattern (Coq Germanicus/Michez at Univ. Caen Normandie-ENSICAEN/CIMAP; Boch at Aix-Marseille Universite/IM2NP -- both established French radiation-effects-testing groups); not independently re-confirmed from the paper's own affiliation block this session -- flag for verifier.

title: Kilopower Project: The KRUSTY Fission Power Experiment and Potential Missions
authors_or_org: Patrick R. McClure, David I. Poston (Los Alamos National Laboratory), Marc A. Gibson, Lee S. Mason, R. Chris Robinson (NASA Glenn Research Center)
claim_supported: Describes the Kilopower Reactor Using Stirling TechnologY (KRUSTY) ground nuclear-test campaign (completed March 2018 at the Nevada National Security Site) -- a 1kWe-class fission power system using Stirling-cycle power conversion, a distinct fission-power architecture/power-conversion technology (Stirling, not Brayton) and power scale (1kWe vs. the 40-100kWe Fission Surface Power program already in this ledger) from L09-013/L09-026/L09-031/L09-032/L09-033.
locator: Nuclear Technology (American Nuclear Society / Taylor & Francis), Vol.206, Issue sup1 (2020)
notes: Direct WebFetch of the tandfonline.com article page returned HTTP 403 this session; full author list, container-title, volume/issue, year, and type=journal-article confirmed independently via Crossref API. Nuclear Technology is the American Nuclear Society's flagship peer-reviewed journal. Complements L09-047 (INL FSP program page, not independently fetched in the prior scout session) and L09-031/L09-033 (NASA/DOE FSP program awards) with a primary, named-author technical paper on the lower-power Kilopower/KRUSTY precursor demonstration.

title: Space Demonstration of All-Solid-State Lithium-Ion Batteries Aboard the International Space Station
authors_or_org: Yu Miyazawa, Takanobu Shimada, Tetsuhito Fuse, Shuhei Shimada, Sousuke Nishiura, Hidetake Okamoto, Tetsuya Okawa, Takeshi Hoshino, Osamu Kawasaki, Hitoshi Naito
claim_supported: Reports an in-space demonstration of all-solid-state lithium-ion batteries mounted on the ISS Exposed Facility for 434 days (562 charge-discharge cycles), showing no significant degradation and confirming ground-based charge-discharge characterization can estimate on-orbit lifetime -- opens a new power-storage sub-niche (batteries) not previously represented in this lane's ledger, which to date covers only power generation/conversion/propulsion hardware.
notes: Direct WebFetch of the mdpi.com article page returned HTTP 403 this session; full 10-author list, container-title, volume/issue/pages, year, and type=journal-article confirmed independently via Crossref API. Geography JP inferred from the all-Japanese author-name pattern and ISS Exposed Facility experiment context (a JAXA-operated platform); not independently confirmed from an affiliation list this session -- flag for verifier.

title: Iodine Electric Propulsion System Thrust Validation: From Numerical Modeling to In-Space Testing
authors_or_org: Oliver Jia-Richards (University of Michigan, Ann Arbor), Trevor Lafleur (University of New South Wales, Canberra)
claim_supported: Validates thrust output of an iodine-fueled gridded-ion-thruster electric-propulsion system (ThrustMe's NPT30-I2 class) by combining numerical/plasma modeling with in-space test data -- an independent-academic (non-vendor) counterpart to L09-102's ThrustMe-authored control-architecture paper, both covering the same iodine-EP sub-niche from complementary angles (thrust physics vs. control/radiation).
locator: Journal of Propulsion and Power (AIAA), Vol.39, No.6, pp.896-904 (2023)
notes: Full author list, container-title, volume/issue/pages, year, type=journal-article confirmed directly via Crossref API this session; AIAA arc.aiaa.org page itself not separately fetched. Geography: Jia-Richards (University of Michigan, US), Lafleur (UNSW Canberra, Australia) -- both affiliations taken from the authors' known university homes, not independently re-confirmed from the paper's own affiliation block this session.

title: Radiation-Hardened DC-DC Converter Design: Systematic Analysis of Single-Event Transient Effects and Voltage Clamping Mitigation Strategy
authors_or_org: Zhongjie Guo, Yapeng Wang, Bin Wen, Hongpeng Dai, Xiaojing Qiang (Xi'an University of Technology, China)
claim_supported: Systematically analyzes single-event-transient (SET) effects in radiation-hardened DC-DC converter designs and proposes a voltage-clamping mitigation strategy -- a primary rad-hard power-converter design/mitigation study (not a device-physics radiation-damage review like L09-014/L09-015), filling a converter-circuit-level gap versus this lane's existing device-level GaN/SiC radiation papers.
locator: Nuclear Science and Engineering (American Nuclear Society / Taylor & Francis), published online 28 Oct 2025, pp.1-14
notes: Direct WebFetch of the tandfonline.com abstract page was not attempted (anticipating the same 403 pattern seen on the related L09-105 Nuclear Technology tandfonline URL this session); full author list, container-title, pages, year, and type=journal-article, plus explicit confirmation that all authors are affiliated with Xi'an University of Technology, China, were obtained directly via Crossref API. Nuclear Science and Engineering is the American Nuclear Society's other flagship peer-reviewed journal (companion to Nuclear Technology, L09-105).

title: Life test research of a 1.35 kW magnetically shielded Hall thruster
authors_or_org: Nan Wu, Qi Liu, Wei Mao, Yanlin Hu, Zhaopu Yao, Yanming Wei
claim_supported: Reports life-test results for a 1.35kW-class magnetically shielded Hall thruster -- a specific thruster-architecture (magnetic-shielding, erosion-mitigation) and power class not previously represented in this lane's ledger, whose existing Hall-thruster records concentrate on PPU/power-electronics topology (L09-001 through L09-008, L09-011) rather than magnetically shielded discharge-channel life testing.
locator: Plasma Science and Technology (IOP Publishing), Vol.27, No.6, article 064012 (2025)
notes: Full author list, container-title, volume/issue/pages, year, type=journal-article confirmed directly via Crossref API this session; the IOPscience article page itself was not separately fetched. Geography CN inferred from the all-Chinese author-name pattern and the journal's hosting institution (Chinese Academy of Sciences-affiliated Hefei Institutes of Physical Science / ASIPP); not independently confirmed from an affiliation list this session -- flag for verifier.

title: 12.5 kW霍尔推力器热设计措施的有效性分析研究 (Effectiveness Analysis of Thermal Design Methods for a 12.5 kW Hall Thruster)
authors_or_org: Sun Mingming, Kong Fanting, Yang Juntai, Li Pei, Wang Shangmin
claim_supported: Evaluates six thermal-design approaches for a 12.5kW-class Hall thruster via finite-element simulation plus experimental validation, finding that combining radiation-heat-exchange blocking with increased emission coefficients ('Method 4 and 6') is the most effective thermal-design optimization -- a thermal-management sub-niche directly relevant to high-power PPU/thruster power-density limits, distinct from L09-028's control-model study (same journal, different research group focus).
locator: 强激光与粒子束 (High Power Laser and Particle Beams), 2025, article 094005
notes: Publisher page (hplpb.com.cn, the same EI-Compendex-indexed Chinese-core journal already established as legitimate for this lane via L09-028) fetched directly this session; title, author list, and key finding confirmed directly from the article page. Author Sun Mingming also appears on L09-028 (2017, ion-thruster control model), indicating a sustained individual research program spanning both ion- and Hall-thruster power/thermal topics.

title: ECSS-E-ST-20-20C -- Space engineering: Electrical design and interface requirements for power supply
authors_or_org: European Cooperation for Space Standardization (ECSS) / European Space Agency
claim_supported: Official ECSS standard (15 April 2016) establishing electrical design and interface requirements for spacecraft power-supply/distribution systems using Latching Current Limiters/Resettable LCLs (LCLs/RLCLs), covering main-bus power distribution to external loads and Heater LCL protection, for science/earth-observation/navigation missions (excludes fuse-based GEO-telecom power distribution). Supported by companion handbook ECSS-E-HB-20-20A.
locator: ECSS-E-ST-20-20C standard page, fetched in full
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session -- fills a documented gap explicitly flagged in this lane's prior scout notes/PROGRESS: 'Standards specific to power (ECSS-E-ST-20-20C) were not independently confirmed this session -- only the general electrical/electronic standard (ECSS-E-ST-20C Rev.2, L09-044) was fetched.' This record directly closes that gap with the power-supply-specific companion standard.

title: NASA SBIR Award: Low Cost Radiation-Hardened Cryocooler Control Electronics for Space Missions
claim_supported: NASA awarded CREARE LLC $849,950 (period of performance June 13, 2023 - June 12, 2025) to develop affordable, radiation-resistant brushless-DC-motor-drive control electronics for spacecraft cryocooler compressors, targeting lunar/LEO mission reliability/hardness specifications at lower cost than fully custom rad-hard parts -- a power/control-electronics sub-niche (instrument-cooler drive electronics) not previously represented in this lane's ledger.
locator: SBIR.gov award page 204151, fetched in full
notes: Directly fetched sbir.gov award page this session; awardee, agency, dollar amount, award period, and abstract confirmed directly. Distinct sub-niche from this lane's existing NASA EP-hardware contracts (AEPS $67M, L09-034; NEXT-C $18.4M, L09-012): this is spacecraft-instrument power/control electronics, not thruster PPU hardware.

title: NASA SBIR Award: Radiation Hardened, Programmable Battery Analog Front-End ASIC
claim_supported: NASA awarded Alphacore, Inc. $899,958 (period of performance July 27, 2024 - July 26, 2026) to develop a radiation-hardened, high-accuracy Battery Analog Front-End (B-AFE) ASIC for spacecraft power systems, incorporating Coulomb counting and electrochemical impedance spectroscopy on SOI process technology rated to exceed 300 krad(Si) -- a battery-management/state-estimation power-electronics sub-niche, complementing L09-106's battery hardware-demonstration record with a power-electronics/monitoring angle.
locator: SBIR.gov award page 212066, fetched in full
notes: Directly fetched sbir.gov award page this session; awardee, agency, dollar amount, award period, and abstract confirmed directly. Alphacore also holds a separate, distinct NASA SBIR award for radiation-tolerant high-voltage (100-1000Vdc) DC-DC converters for lunar/Mars missions per WebSearch context this session, but that award's specific sbir.gov page/ID and dollar figure could not be independently located/fetched this session (a 404 was returned for one candidate award ID) -- not included as a separate record to avoid an unconfirmed entry.

title: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Form 10-K -- Space Systems segment (SolAero solar power products, reaction wheels, spacecraft battery power systems)
claim_supported: Rocket Lab's audited FY2025 Form 10-K describes its Space Systems reportable segment (part of $601.8M FY2025 total revenue, vs. $436.2M FY2024 and $244.6M FY2023) as including vertically-integrated space solar cells, Coverglass Interconnected Cells (CICs), and solar-array products (via the January 2022 SolAero Technologies Corp. acquisition) for LEO/MEO/GEO/interplanetary missions, plus motor-driven reaction-wheel flywheels (3 mNms-12 Nms) and high-capacity, high-voltage spacecraft batteries -- direct evidence of a vertically-integrated commercial supplier of spacecraft power-generation and power-storage hardware, a company/incumbent-map data point not previously in this lane's ledger.
locator: Rocket Lab USA, Inc. Form 10-K (FY2025, filed 2026), Space Systems segment description, as quoted via StockTitan SEC-filing summary
notes: IMPORTANT LIMITATION: direct WebFetch of the primary SEC.gov 10-K filing (sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1819994/000181999426000013/rklb-20251231.htm) returned HTTP 403 this session (SEC.gov blocks the fetch tool's request pattern); the quoted text above was obtained by fetching a third-party SEC-filing-aggregator page (stocktitan.net) that reproduces verbatim passages from the primary 10-K. This is disclosed transparently per claim-discipline rules; canonical_key points to the primary SEC filing for identity purposes, while the fetched/evidentiary url is the stocktitan page actually retrieved this session. Verifier should attempt an independent primary-source fetch (e.g., via an authenticated tool) before treating financial figures as fully confirmed.

title: AIAA S-122 -- Electrical Power Systems for Unmanned Spacecraft
authors_or_org: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA); endorsed by NASA Office of the Chief Engineer
claim_supported: AIAA S-122 (published 2007; endorsed by NASA's Office of the Chief Engineer on November 3, 2017, as a non-mandatory reference standard) establishes design practices and verification/validation requirements for unmanned-spacecraft electrical power systems, primarily targeting earth-orbiting satellites using photovoltaic/battery power while remaining applicable to alternative power-generation/storage methods, aiming at robust EPS design for high-reliability missions.
locator: standards.nasa.gov AIAA-S-122 standard record page, fetched in full
notes: Directly fetched and confirmed this session. This is the US/AIAA-side counterpart to the European ECSS-E-ST-20-20C (L09-111) and ECSS-E-ST-20C Rev.2 (L09-044) power-system standards -- a useful US-vs-Europe standards-regime comparison point for this lane's 'US vs Asia/Europe differences' section, not previously represented (this ledger had only the European ECSS standards before this session).
